h1.visual_description: Not present in sample, but typically large, centered, all-caps or bold serif at chapter starts.

h2.visual_description: Not present in sample; likely centered serif with significant leading.

h3.visual_description: Small caps or bold serif, often used for sub-sections within chapters.

visual_matchers.semantic_meaning: Image caption: Small-caps 'FIGURE' followed by number, roman text for description, and smaller all-caps for publication metadata.
visual_matchers.example_text: FIGURE 4.11 A. Birrell, Sir Robert Shirley [graphic]. Engraving on wove paper. PUBLISHED BY EDWARD HARDING, LONDON, 1799

visual_matchers.semantic_meaning: Footnote: Smaller serif font at the base of the page, separated by a horizontal rule, beginning with a numerical index.
visual_matchers.example_text: 86 Elaine Freedgood, “Introduction: Reading Things,” in The Idea in Things...
The Arabian Nights in Historical Context

markdown_grammar.citation_format: Chicago-style footnotes with superscript numbering in-text and corresponding entries at the footer.
